description: Lock medical_triage_practices to source-backed modern battlefield casualty sorting, not a Classical-era supply-store practice.

checks.reason: The scoped claim is anchored to Larrey-era and later battlefield casualty sorting systems rather than Classical medicine.
Modern triage emerged within military medical practice.
Larrey-style casualty sorting operated through evacuation logistics, medical teams, wagons, and mobile hospitals.
Triage sorts casualties across constrained treatment echelons such as aid stations, field hospitals, and general hospitals.
State medical supply storage is not the source-backed causal boundary for modern battlefield casualty sorting.
